headline: Lululemon's next CEO knows how to get it 'back on track': Analyst
summary: Lululemon Athletica (LULU) stock is dropping in Thursday's extended hours after the athleisure brand reported a slight revenue beat and trimmed its full-year revenue forecast. Morningstar senior equity analyst David Swartz explains how incoming CEO Heidi O'Neill will know how to get the company and customer shopping experiences "back on track." O'Neill will start in her chief executive role this September after her non-compete agreement with Nike (NKE) expires.

content.title: Lululemon's next CEO knows how to get it 'back on track': Analyst
content.description:
Lululemon Athletica (LULU) stock is dropping in Thursday's extended hours after the athleisure brand reported a slight revenue beat and trimmed its full-year revenue forecast.

Morningstar senior equity analyst David Swartz explains how incoming CEO Heidi O'Neill will know how to get the company and customer shopping experiences "back on track."

O'Neill will start in her chief executive role this September after her non-compete agreement with Nike (NKE) expires.
content.summary: Lululemon Athletica (LULU) stock is dropping in Thursday's extended hours after the athleisure brand reported a slight revenue beat and trimmed its full-year revenue forecast. Morningstar senior equity analyst David Swartz explains how incoming CEO Heidi O'Neill will know how to get the company and customer shopping experiences "back on track." O'Neill will start in her chief executive role this September after her non-compete agreement with Nike (NKE) expires.

headline: Lululemon (NASDAQ:LULU) Exceeds Q1 CY2026 Expectations But Stock Drops
summary: Athletic apparel retailer Lululemon (NASDAQ:LULU) announced better-than-expected revenue in Q1 CY2026, with sales up 4.3% year on year to $2.47 billion. On the other hand, next quarter’s revenue guidance of $2.46 billion was less impressive, coming in 5.1% below analysts’ estimates. Its GAAP profit of $1.69 per share was 1% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

content.title: Lululemon (NASDAQ:LULU) Exceeds Q1 CY2026 Expectations But Stock Drops
content.summary: Athletic apparel retailer Lululemon (NASDAQ:LULU) announced better-than-expected revenue in Q1 CY2026, with sales up 4.3% year on year to $2.47 billion. On the other hand, next quarter’s revenue guidance of $2.46 billion was less impressive, coming in 5.1% below analysts’ estimates. Its GAAP profit of $1.69 per share was 1% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Lululemon Issues Weaker Outlook. What It Means for the Stock.
summary: Lululemon  Athletica reported better-than-expected first-quarter results but a weaker outlook for the second quarter and the full year, sending its shares lower in after-hours trading.  Shares of Lululemon were down more than 10% after closing down 0.9% at $124.92 in regular trading.  The specialty athleticwear, footwear, and accessories company said sales for the fiscal quarter ended May 3 rose 4% to $2.5 billion, above the $2.43 billion Wall Street was estimating.

content.title: Lululemon Issues Weaker Outlook. What It Means for the Stock.
content.summary: Lululemon  Athletica reported better-than-expected first-quarter results but a weaker outlook for the second quarter and the full year, sending its shares lower in after-hours trading.  Shares of Lululemon were down more than 10% after closing down 0.9% at $124.92 in regular trading.  The specialty athleticwear, footwear, and accessories company said sales for the fiscal quarter ended May 3 rose 4% to $2.5 billion, above the $2.43 billion Wall Street was estimating.

headline: Lululemon (LULU) Q1 2026 Earnings Transcript
summary: Before we get started, I would like to take this opportunity to remind you that our remarks today will include forward looking statements reflecting management's current forecast of certain aspects of Lululemon's future.  As well as to Laura Gentile, and Mark Maurer, who will join the board following our annual meeting later this month.

content.summary: Before we get started, I would like to take this opportunity to remind you that our remarks today will include forward looking statements reflecting management's current forecast of certain aspects of Lululemon's future.  As well as to Laura Gentile, and Mark Maurer, who will join the board following our annual meeting later this month.
